questions.text: Anything that can be used to satisfy a need is a ________.
questions.explanation: Utility or usability is what makes an object or substance a resource.

questions.text: What makes an object a resource?
questions.explanation: A resource is defined by its ability to satisfy human needs (Utility).

questions.text: Resources that are drawn from nature and used without much modification are called:
questions.explanation: Air, water, soil, and minerals are natural resources.

questions.text: Resources which have a limited stock are called:
questions.explanation: Coal, petroleum, and natural gas take thousands of years to renew and thus are non-renewable.

questions.text: Resources that are found everywhere like the air we breathe are called:
questions.explanation: Ubiquitous means found everywhere.

questions.text: Resources which are found only in certain places are called:
questions.explanation: Iron ore or copper are examples of localized resources.

questions.text: Using resources carefully and giving them time to get renewed is called:
questions.explanation: Conservation ensures resources don't run out.

questions.text: Balancing the need to use resources and also conserve them for the future is called:
questions.explanation: Sustainable development ensures progress without compromising the needs of future generations.

questions.text: Which of these is a human-made resource?
questions.explanation: Buildings, bridges, roads, and medicines are created by humans using natural substances.

questions.text: People are a ________ resource.
questions.explanation: Human skills, knowledge, and technology make people themselves a resource.

questions.text: Improving the quality of people's skills so that they are able to create more resources is known as:
questions.explanation: Education and health help in making people a valuable resource.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a principle of sustainable development?
options: Respect and care for all forms of life
Minimize the depletion of natural resources
Maximize the use of non-renewable resources
Change personal attitude and practices toward the environment
questions.explanation: Sustainable development aims to reduce, not maximize, the depletion of resources.

questions.text: Abiotic resources are derived from ________.
questions.explanation: Rocks, minerals, and soils are abiotic resources.

questions.text: Biotic resources are derived from ________.
questions.explanation: Plants and animals are biotic resources.

questions.text: The exclusive right over any idea or invention is called:
questions.explanation: A patent protects an invention legally.

questions.text: Time and ________ are two important factors that can change substances into resources.
questions.explanation: Needs of people lead to the discovery of resources using technology over time.

questions.text: High speed winds were a potential resource 200 years ago, but today they are a/an ________ resource.
questions.explanation: Actual resources are those whose quantity is known and which are being used currently.

questions.text: The quantity of potential resources is ________.
questions.explanation: Potential resources are those whose entire quantity may not be known and are not being used at present.

questions.text: The dark soils of the Deccan plateau in Maharashtra are ________ resources.
questions.explanation: They are being actively used for agriculture.

questions.text: Solar and wind energy are examples of ________ resources.
questions.explanation: Renewable resources get replenished quickly and are not easily exhausted by human activity.

questions.text: Resource distribution is unequal on Earth because of differences in ________.
questions.explanation: Physical factors vary greatly across the planet.

questions.text: A substance becomes a resource only when its ________ is discovered.
questions.explanation: Utility makes something valuable for human use.

questions.text: Which of these is an example of an actual resource?
options: Coal in Ruhr region of Germany
questions.explanation: The coal in Ruhr is currently being mined and its quantity is known.

questions.text: Which of these is NOT a natural resource?
questions.explanation: Roads are human-made resources.

questions.text: Value means ________.
questions.explanation: Some resources have economic value, while others have aesthetic value.

questions.text: Natural resources are classified into different groups depending upon their level of development and ________.
questions.explanation: Classification is based on development, use, origin, stock and distribution.

questions.text: Resources found in a region but not yet used are ________ resources.
questions.explanation: For example, Uranium found in Ladakh is a potential resource.

questions.text: Petroleum is a ________ resource.
questions.explanation: Petroleum stocks are limited and cannot be replaced in a short time.

questions.text: Stock of resource is the amount of resources ________.
questions.explanation: Stock refers to the total quantity available.

questions.text: What is the most important resource on Earth?
questions.explanation: Humans create all other resources through their ideas and technology.

questions.text: To 'reduce consumption, recycle and reuse' is part of ________.
questions.explanation: The 3 Rs are essential for saving the environment.

questions.text: The future of our planet and its people is linked with our ability to maintain the ________.
questions.explanation: Conserving nature ensures that our life support system (air, water, soil) remains intact.

questions.text: Development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the future is ________ development.
questions.explanation: This is the core definition of sustainable development.

questions.text: Uranium found in Ladakh is an example of ________ resource.
questions.explanation: We know it's there but we aren't using it yet.

questions.text: Iron ore becomes a resource only after humans learn to ________.
questions.explanation: Technology is required to transform a substance like ore into a useful resource.

questions.text: All living things are ________ resources.
questions.explanation: Biotic means living.

questions.text: Renewable resources are also known as ________ resources.
questions.explanation: They do not run out easily (e.g., solar).

questions.text: Which of these is a localized resource?
questions.explanation: Copper is found only in specific geographical locations.

questions.text: A beautiful landscape has ________ value.
questions.explanation: It doesn't provide money directly, but it provides psychological satisfaction.

questions.text: The use of resources depends on ________.
questions.explanation: Multiple factors determine how and why we use resources.
